questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer is monitoring and handling groups of Amazon EC2 instances using AWS Systems Manager. Whenever there are batch operations such as maintenance and OS patching on these instances, the Engineer can rely on the Systems Manager to automatically perform these activities.

What other tasks can the Administrator perform with automation in Systems Manager? (Select TWO.)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What automation capabilities does AWS Systems Manager provide beyond batch operations?
choices: Set synchronized EC2 instance restart times even without proper user access to some instances.
Allow unlimited concurrent automation executions without duration limits.
Design Automation documents that are securely tied to the user and cannot be shared to others.
Create custom workflows or use pre-defined workflows maintained by AWS.
Receive notifications about automation tasks and workflows by using Amazon EventBridge.
correctAnswers: Create custom workflows or use pre-defined workflows maintained by AWS.
Receive notifications about automation tasks and workflows by using Amazon EventBridge.
questions.explanation:
AWS Systems Manager gives you visibility and control of your infrastructure on AWS. Systems Manager provides a unified user interface so you can view operational data from multiple AWS services and allows you to automate operational tasks across your AWS resources. Systems Manager simplifies resource and application management, shortens the time to detect and resolve operational problems, and makes it easy to operate and manage your infrastructure securely at scale.

AWS Systems Manager

These are the automation capabilities of Systems Manager that you can perform on EC2 instances:

– Build automation to configure and manage instances and AWS resources.

– Create custom runbooks or use pre-defined runbooks maintained by AWS.

– Receive notifications about Automation tasks and runbooks by using Amazon EventBridge.

– Monitor Automation progress and details by using the AWS Systems Manager console.

Hence, the correct answers are:

– Create custom workflows or use pre-defined workflows maintained by AWS.

– Receive notifications about automation tasks and workflows by using Amazon EventBridge.

The option that says: Design Automation documents that are securely tied to the user and cannot be shared to others is incorrect because you can typically create best practices for resource management in automation documents and easily share the documents across AWS Regions and groups. You can also constrain the allowed values for the parameters that the document accepts.

The option that says: Set synchronized EC2 instance restart times even without proper user access to some instances is incorrect because access to Systems Manager requires credentials. Those credentials must have permission to access AWS resources for different tasks. You can have valid credentials to authenticate your requests, but unless you have permission, you cannot primarily create or access Systems Manager resources.

The option that says: Allow unlimited concurrent automation executions without duration limits is incorrect because Systems Manager has service limits when it comes to concurrently executing automations (25) and maximum duration an automation execution can run (12 hrs).

questions.question:
A popular sports website, which provides news, opinions, and videos on various sports events around the world, is hosted in AWS. The website’s architecture has an Auto Scaling group of On-Demand EC2 instances across multiple Availability Zones with an Application Load Balancer (ALB) to evenly distribute the incoming traffic. Lately, online readers have been giving the site a low rating because of the slow loading time of their site. Due to the upcoming FIFA World cup competition, it is expected that the traffic will continue to surge in the coming weeks ahead.

Which of the following services can be used as a cost-effective solution to scale and improve the site’s loading time?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service cost-effectively improves website loading time for global users?
choices: Set up VPC peering
Set up a Network Load Balancer instead of an ALB
Set up a CloudFront distribution
Enable Enhanced Networking
correctAnswers: Set up a CloudFront distribution
questions.explanation:
Amazon CloudFront is a fast content delivery network (CDN) service that securely delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs to customers globally with low latency, high transfer speeds, all within a developer-friendly environment. CloudFront is integrated with AWS – both physical locations that are directly connected to the AWS global infrastructure, as well as other AWS services.

If your website takes a long time to load, your users might abandon it before completing their transaction, affecting your revenue. You can use a content delivery network (CDN) like Amazon CloudFront to improve the performance of your website by securely delivering data, videos, applications, and APIs to customers globally with low latency and high transfer speeds.

To improve performance, you can simply configure your website’s traffic to be delivered over CloudFront’s globally distributed edge network by setting up CloudFront distribution.

Hence the correct answer is: Set up a CloudFront distribution.

The option that says: Set up a Network Load Balancer instead of an ALB is incorrect because a Network Load Balancer is more ideal for load balancing of TCP traffic and for handling millions of requests per second while maintaining ultra-low latencies. Though it may improve the performance of the site, this is still not a scalable solution to provide faster response time for clients around the globe.

The option that says: Enable Enhanced Networking is incorrect, as Enhanced Networking is mainly used to allow high-performance networking capabilities between EC2 instances. This won’t affect the speed of user traffic coming from/into the instances.

The option that says: Set up VPC peering is incorrect. VPC peering is simply a network connection that allows VPCs to communicate privately with one another. This option will not resolve the issue.

questions.question:
An office supply store chain currently runs a five-day sale on its e-commerce web application using burstable EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling Group (ASG) fronted by an Application Load Balancer (ALB). The ASG scales out when the CPU Utilization reaches 70%.

Users report slow load times even though the administrator observes a 20% CPU utilization across all instances.

Which solution can resolve the issue in the most cost-effective way?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you resolve slow load times when CPU utilization is low but users experience latency?
choices: Enable unlimited mode for all the burstable instances in the ASG.
Relocate the infrastructure to an AWS Region closer to the majority of the users.
Create an Amazon CloudFront distribution to decrease direct network traffic to the ALB.
Artificially increase the CPU Utilization by decreasing the desired size of the ASG. The fewer instance you have in the ASG, the higher the CPU Utilization will be.
correctAnswers: Create an Amazon CloudFront distribution to decrease direct network traffic to the ALB.
questions.explanation:
Amazon CloudFront is a fast content delivery network (CDN) service that securely delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs to customers globally with low latency, high transfer speeds, all within a developer-friendly environment.

CloudFront works seamlessly with any AWS origin, such as Amazon S3, Amazon EC2, Elastic Load Balancing, or with any custom HTTP origin.

In the scenario, users are experiencing slow load times despite low CPU utilization across the instances. This could indicate that the users are geographically far from the region where the instances are located. In such cases, latency caused by the distance between users and the instances can result in slower content delivery and degraded user experience. To address this issue, you can use Amazon CloudFront.

Amazon CloudFront is a web service that speeds up the distribution of your content, such as.html,.css,.js, and image files, to your users. CloudFront delivers your content through a worldwide network of data centers called edge locations. When a user requests content that you’re serving with CloudFront, the request is routed to the edge location that provides the lowest latency (time delay), so that content is delivered with the best possible performance.

Hence, the correct answer is: Create an Amazon CloudFront distribution to decrease direct network traffic to the ALB.

The option that says: Enable unlimited mode for all the burstable instances in the ASG is incorrect because this will only help you sustain high CPU utilization to handle more workloads but it will not improve the load time.

The option that says: Relocate the infrastructure to an AWS Region closer to the majority of the users is incorrect. This approach may not be the most cost-effective solution, as it would require migrating the entire infrastructure. Additionally, it might not address the needs of users in other regions.

The option that says: Artificially increase the CPU Utilization by decreasing the desired size of the ASG. The fewer instance you have in the ASG, the higher the CPU Utilization will be is incorrect because this will just cause more problems since the desired ASG size is reduced.

questions.question:
An insurance company plans to migrate its online customer portal, which uses a relational database, from its on-premises infrastructure to AWS. The CloudOps Engineer is instructed to do the migration in a cost-effective and manageable manner.

Which of the following services would the Engineer choose for hosting the database in AWS?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service is best for hosting a migrated relational database in a cost-effective way?
choices: AWS Storage Gateway
Amazon RDS
Amazon ElastiCache
Amazon Elastic File System (EFS)
correctAnswers: Amazon RDS
questions.explanation:
Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) is a web service that makes it easier to set up, operate, and scale a relational database in the cloud. It provides cost-efficient, resizable capacity for an industry-standard relational database and manages common database administration tasks.

To migrate a database from on-premises to AWS, you need to use AWS Database Migration Service. The source database remains fully operational during the migration, minimizing downtime to applications that rely on the database. Among the given options, Amazon RDS is the most suitable service to migrate a relational database from on-premises infrastructure.

Hence, the correct answer is: Amazon RDS.

AWS Storage Gateway is incorrect because this is not a database service. Storage Gateway is a service that allows on-premises access to virtually unlimited cloud storage.

Amazon ElastiCache is incorrect because ElastiCache is primarily used as an in-memory caching service to improve database performance.

Amazon Elastic File System (EFS) is incorrect. Amazon EFS is generally used as a distributed file system that allows multiple clients to access files concurrently.

questions.question:
A large business technology company heavily uses AWS to run its suite of cloud-based enterprise resource planning (ERP) applications across multiple business units. To save on costs, they are planning to run automated start/stop scripts that turn off EC2 instances in the development and UAT environments during non-business hours.

Which of the following is the MOST suitable feature to use for this scenario?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What EC2 feature is best for filtering instances targeted by automated start/stop scripts?
choices: Instance user data
EC2Rescue
Instance metadata
Tags
correctAnswers: Tags
questions.explanation:
Resource or service-specific tags are often used to filter resources during infrastructure automation activities. Automation tags are used to opt in or opt-out of automated tasks or to identify specific versions of resources to archive, update, or delete. For example, many customers run automated start/stop scripts that turn off development environments during non-business hours to reduce costs. In this scenario, Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instance tags are a simple way to identify the specific development instances to opt-out of this action. For scripts used to locate and delete stale, out-of-date, or rolling Amazon EBS snapshots, snapshot tags can add an extra dimension of search criteria.

Hence, the correct answer is Tags.

Instance metadata is incorrect because this is just data about your instance that you can use to configure or manage the running instance.

Instance user data is incorrect because this is a user-defined parameter or script for configuring your EC2 instance.

EC2Rescue is incorrect because this is just an easy-to-use tool that is primarily used to diagnose and troubleshoot possible problems in your Amazon EC2 instances. It is valuable for collecting log files and troubleshooting issues and also proactively searching for possible areas of concern.

questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer is setting up an Amazon CloudWatch Dashboard, which should track the memory and swap usage of all Linux Amazon EC2 Instances in Amazon VPC. There will be an upcoming IT audit, and this should be done as soon as possible.

Which of the following options should be implemented to fulfill this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you enable CloudWatch to track memory and swap usage on EC2 instances?
choices: Install CloudWatch Agent on the EC2 Instances.
Enable detailed monitoring on the EC2 instances.
Enable basic monitoring on the EC2 instances.
Register for a CloudWatch account to enable metrics monitoring.
correctAnswers: Install CloudWatch Agent on the EC2 Instances.
questions.explanation:
Amazon CloudWatch is a monitoring service for AWS cloud resources and the applications you run on AWS. You can use Amazon CloudWatch to collect and track metrics, collect and monitor log files, and set alarms. You can use Amazon CloudWatch to gain system-wide visibility into resource utilization, application performance, and operational health. You can use these insights to react and keep your application running smoothly.

Amazon CloudWatch

You can use the CloudWatch agent to collect both system metrics and log files from Amazon EC2 instances and on-premises servers. The agent supports both Windows Server and Linux and enables you to select the metrics to be collected, including sub-resource metrics such as per-CPU core and memory usage.

Hence, the correct answer is: Install CloudWatch Agent on the EC2 Instances.

The option that says: Register for a CloudWatch account to enable metrics monitoring is incorrect because you don’t need to explicitly register for a CloudWatch account.

The option that says: Enable detailed monitoring on the EC2 instances is incorrect because detailed monitoring only helps you to send metric data for your instance to CloudWatch in 1-minute periods.

The option that says: Enable basic monitoring on the EC2 instances is incorrect because this simply helps your EC2 instances send metric data to CloudWatch in 5-minute intervals.

questions.question:
A large technology company owns several IT Consulting firms and has individual AWS accounts. As the CloudOps Engineer, you are responsible for setting up their cloud architecture, ensuring that they are able to centrally manage policies and billing for their multiple AWS accounts.

Which of the following options would you implement to satisfy this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service enables centralized policy management and billing across multiple AWS accounts?
choices: Use Consolidated Billing.
Use a separate IAM policy on each account.
Use AWS Organizations to connect all of their AWS accounts.
Set up an IAM group for each IT consulting firm.
correctAnswers: Use AWS Organizations to connect all of their AWS accounts.
questions.explanation:
AWS Organizations offers policy-based management for multiple AWS accounts. With Organizations, you can create groups of accounts, automate account creation, apply and manage policies for those groups. Organizations enables you to centrally manage policies across multiple accounts, without requiring custom scripts and manual processes.

Using AWS Organizations, you can create Service Control Policies (SCPs) that centrally control AWS service use across multiple AWS accounts. You can also use Organizations to help automate the creation of new accounts through APIs. Organizations helps simplify the billing for multiple accounts by enabling you to set up a single payment method for all the accounts in your organization through consolidated billing. AWS Organizations is available to all AWS customers at no additional charge.

Hence, the correct answer is: Use AWS Organizations to connect all of their AWS accounts.

The option that says: Use Consolidated Billing is incorrect because this is just for consolidating billing and payment for multiple AWS accounts.

The option that says: Use a separate IAM policy on each account is incorrect because you can’t attach IAM policies to AWS accounts. They can only be attached to IAM identities (users, groups to which users belong, or roles).

The option that says: Set up an IAM group for each IT consulting firm is incorrect because an IAM group is only a collection of IAM users. To manage multiple AWS accounts, you need to create organizational units in the AWS Organization.

questions.question:
A news-based pay television channel has a requirement of load balancing the incoming TCP traffic to their website. To maintain performance, it should handle millions of requests per second while maintaining ultra-low latencies.

Which of the following should you add in their current cloud architecture?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which load balancer handles millions of TCP requests per second with ultra-low latency?
choices: Amazon Route 53
Auto Scaling Group
Application Load Balancer
Network Load Balancer
correctAnswers: Network Load Balancer
questions.explanation:
Elastic Load Balancing automatically distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets, such as Amazon EC2 instances, containers, IP addresses, and Lambda functions. It can handle the varying load of your application traffic in a single Availability Zone or across multiple Availability Zones.

Network Load Balancer is best suited for load balancing of TCP traffic where extreme performance is required. Operating at the connection level (Layer 4), Network Load Balancer routes traffic to targets within Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC) and is capable of handling millions of requests per second while maintaining ultra-low latencies. Network Load Balancer is also optimized to handle sudden and volatile traffic patterns.

Hence, the correct answer is Network Load Balancer.

Application Load Balancer is incorrect because it cannot handle TCP or Layer 4 connections, only Layer 7 (HTTP and HTTPS).

Auto Scaling Group is incorrect because this does not allow you to balance traffic to your application. It just helps you ensure that your application has the right amount of capacity to handle traffic demand.

Amazon Route 53 is incorrect because this is a highly available and scalable cloud Domain Name System (DNS) web service and not a load balancer.

questions.question:
A website application is hosted on-premises, and a CloudOps Engineer plans to consider using Route53 options to make the application highly available. The application is deployed on a primary server (Alpha) and a secondary passive server (Delta). She needs to configure Route 53 to direct traffic to the Alpha server if a health check returns 2xx or 3xx HTTP status codes. Else, other traffic should route towards the Delta server. The routing policies, failover record types, and record IDs are configured correctly for both servers.

What step should the CloudOps Engineer perform next?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What record type and health check should Route 53 use for active-passive failover to on-premises servers?
choices: Create an A record for both servers. Then, associate the new records with a Route 53 TCP health check.
Create an alias record for both servers. Set the Evaluate Target Health setting to Yes. Then, associate the records with a Route 53 HTTP health check.
Create an A record for both Alpha and Delta servers. Then, associate the new records with a Route 53 HTTP health check.
Create an alias record for both Alpha and Delta servers. Set the Evaluate Target Health setting to Yes. Then, associate the records with a Route 53 TCP health check.
correctAnswers: Create an A record for both Alpha and Delta servers. Then, associate the new records with a Route 53 HTTP health check.
questions.explanation:
To create an active-passive failover configuration with one primary record and one secondary record, you just create the records and specify Failover for the routing policy. When the primary resource is healthy, Route 53 responds to DNS queries using the primary record. When the primary resource is unhealthy, Route 53 responds to DNS queries using the secondary record.

Check the health of EC2 instances and other resources (non-alias records)

If you’re routing traffic to resources that you can’t create alias records for, such as EC2 instances, you create a record and a health check for each resource. Then you associate each health check with the applicable record. Health checks regularly check the health of the corresponding resources, and Route 53 routes traffic only to the resources that health checks report as healthy.

Here’s an overview of how you configure Route 53 to check the health of your resources in simple configurations:

– You identify the resources that you want Route 53 to monitor. For example, you might want to monitor all the HTTP servers that respond to requests for example.com.

– You create health checks for the resources that you can’t create alias records for, such as EC2 instances or servers in your own data center. You specify how to send health-checking requests to the resource: which protocol to use (HTTP, HTTPS, or TCP), which IP address and port to use, and, for HTTP/HTTPS health checks, a domain name and path.

Hence, the correct answer is: Create an A record for both Alpha and Delta servers. Then, associate the new records with a Route 53 HTTP health check.

The option that says: Create an alias record for both Alpha and Delta servers. Set the Evaluate Target Health setting to Yes. Then, associate the records with a Route 53 TCP health check is incorrect because an alias record only allows you to route traffic to selected AWS resources or to another record. It will not help you route traffic to an on-premises server.

The option that says: Create an alias record for both servers. Set the Evaluate Target Health setting to Yes. Then, associate the records with a Route 53 HTTP health check is incorrect because an alias record can’t route traffic to servers on your own data center. It is primarily used to route traffic to selected AWS resources or to another record in the same hosted zone.

The option that says: Create an A record for both servers. Then, associate the new records with a Route 53 TCP health check is incorrect because you don’t need a TCP health check. It is stated in the scenario that health checks should return HTTP status codes.

questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer needs to assess the behavior of AWS resources and identify potential security issues in the cloud architecture. The engineer also deploys Amazon CloudWatch Synthetics canaries to monitor the availability of the application endpoints. The engineer is also required to produce a report on multiple activities that are occurring within the Amazon EC2 instances and gather data on these targets, such as details of communication with other AWS services, use of secure channels, details of the running processes, network traffic among the running processes, and many others.

Which of the following services will help the engineer assess the EC2 instances?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service analyzes EC2 instance behavior and identifies potential security issues?
choices: Amazon Macie
AWS Systems Manager
AWS Trusted Advisor
Amazon Inspector
correctAnswers: Amazon Inspector
questions.explanation:
Amazon Inspector enables you to analyze the behavior of your AWS resources and helps you to identify potential security issues. You can create an assessment template and launch a security assessment run of this target.

Define an assessment on Amazon Inspector

During the assessment run, the network, file system, and process activity within the specified target are monitored, and a wide set of activity and configuration data is collected. The collected data is correlated, analyzed, and compared to a set of security rules specified in the assessment template. A completed assessment run produces a list of findings – potential security problems of various severity.

Hence, the correct answer is: Amazon Inspector.

AWS Trusted Advisor is incorrect because it only provides you with advice on how to optimize and secure your AWS environment, but it does not produce the details that you need for your report.

Amazon Macie is incorrect because it is used to discover, classify, and protect sensitive data in AWS, such as personally identifiable information or intellectual property. However, it does not check for other security details, such as network traffic and running processes.

AWS Systems Manager is incorrect because it just provides you with a unified user interface with a centralized and consistent way to gather operational insights and carry out routine management tasks. It does not assess the security of your EC2 instances the way Inspector does.

questions.question:
Large amounts of transactions are going through your banking system into your EC2 instances, causing degradation in its overall performance. After monitoring the event, you found out that the root cause of the problem is the data transferring to your EBS volumes. This is caused by a high volume of transactions exceeding your bandwidth capacity.

What would be a cost-effective solution to optimize EBS performance?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What is the most cost-effective way to optimize EBS I/O performance when bandwidth is exceeded?
choices: Increase the number of EBS volumes for your instances.
If the instance type of your instance does not support EBS optimization, change your instance type to one that supports it.
Throttle the incoming traffic to enable your instances and EBS volumes to cope up with the volume of transactions.
Increase the number of instances supporting your system.
correctAnswers: If the instance type of your instance does not support EBS optimization, change your instance type to one that supports it.
questions.explanation:
An Amazon EBS–optimized instance uses an optimized configuration stack and provides additional, dedicated capacity for Amazon EBS I/O. This optimization provides the best performance for your EBS volumes by minimizing contention between Amazon EBS I/O and other traffic from your instance.

For applications with spiky Amazon EBS demand, you can right-size your instances based on your CPU and memory requirements and still meet your EBS-optimized instance performance requirements. This higher performance also enables you to speed up sections of your workflow dependent on EBS-optimized instance performance. Faster workflows result in quicker job completions and improved resource utilization. The burst capability ultimately enables you to reduce costs by right-sizing your instance and improving total resource usage.

Hence, the correct answer is: If the instance type of your instance does not support EBS optimization, change your instance type to one that supports it.

The option that says: Increase the number of EBS volumes for your instances is incorrect because you should try to enable EBS optimization first before increasing the number of EBS volumes that you have, and verify if EBS optimization will be enough to meet your performance requirements.

The option that says: Increase the number of instances supporting your system is incorrect because you should run an instance that supports EBS optimization first to verify if EBS optimization can solve your performance requirements.

The option that says: Throttle the incoming traffic to enable your instances and EBS volumes to cope up with the volume of transactions is incorrect since throttling your network might cause your transactions to pile up and affect your banking operations, which is counterproductive from what you are trying to solve.

questions.question:
A company is providing cloud-based accounting software to various clients. Recently, the IT Operations team have identified a series of infrastructure layer attacks to the VPC such as SYN floods, UDP floods and other DDoS attacks. To protect the data of the clients and the business continuity of the company, the CloudOps Engineer was instructed to fortify their current cloud infrastructure.

Which of the following is the best service that the Engineer can use to mitigate these kinds of attacks?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service best mitigates SYN floods, UDP floods, and other DDoS attacks on a VPC?
choices: AWS WAF
AWS Shield Advanced
AWS Systems Manager
AWS Firewall Manager
correctAnswers: AWS Shield Advanced
questions.explanation:
AWS Shield is a managed Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) protection service that safeguards applications running on AWS. AWS Shield provides always-on detection and automatic inline mitigations that minimize application downtime and latency, so there is no need to engage AWS Support to benefit from DDoS protection. There are two tiers of AWS Shield – Standard and Advanced.

For higher levels of protection against attacks targeting your applications running on Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2), Elastic Load Balancing (ELB), Amazon CloudFront, and Amazon Route 53 resources, you can subscribe to AWS Shield Advanced. In addition to the network and transport layer protections that come with Standard, AWS Shield Advanced provides additional detection and mitigation against large and sophisticated DDoS attacks, near real-time visibility into attacks, and integration with AWS WAF, a web application firewall. AWS Shield Advanced also gives you 24×7 access to the AWS DDoS Response Team (DRT) and protection against DDoS-related spikes in your Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2), Elastic Load Balancing(ELB), Amazon CloudFront, and Amazon Route 53 charges.

Hence, the correct answer is: AWS Shield Advanced.

AWS Firewall Manager is incorrect because AWS Firewall Manager is mainly used to simplify your AWS WAF administration and maintenance tasks across multiple accounts and resources. It does not protect your VPC against DDoS attacks.

AWS WAF is incorrect. Even though AWS WAF can help you block common attack patterns to your VPC, such as SQL injection or cross-site scripting, this is still not enough to withstand DDoS attacks. It is better to use AWS Shield in this scenario.

AWS Systems Manager is incorrect because AWS Systems Manager is just a management service that helps you automatically collect software inventory, apply OS patches, and create system images, but this does not protect your VPC against DDoS attacks.

questions.question:
A company recently adopted a hybrid cloud architecture that requires it to migrate some of its on-premises web applications to AWS. The CloudOps Engineer is instructed to create an AWS CloudFormation template that automatically provisions AWS resources such as Amazon EC2 instances, ELB, and Amazon RDS instances. After running the stack using the CLI, it successfully launched the EC2 instances and the ELB, but it failed to create a new RDS instance. The template does not specify the OnFailure policy, so CloudFormation will use its default behavior rather than an action like DO_NOTHING.

In this scenario, what will the CloudFormation do by default?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What is CloudFormation's default behavior when a resource fails to create during stack deployment?
choices: By default, the CloudFormation will complete the stack creation since the EC2 and Amazon EBS resources were already launched.
By default, the CloudFormation will roll back the stack.
By default, the CloudFormation will roll back and delete the stack.
By default, there will be a Wait Condition that will pause the stack creation and prompt the user to acknowledge the failure.
correctAnswers: By default, the CloudFormation will roll back the stack.
questions.explanation:
The create-stack CloudFormation CLI command creates a stack as specified in the template. After the call completes successfully, the stack creation starts. You can check the status of the stack via the DescribeStacks API.

CloudFormation StackSets deploying across multiple accounts and regions.

It has an --on-failure optional parameter which determines what action will be taken if stack creation fails. Its default value is ROLLBACK which means that the CloudFormation service will automatically rollback the stack in the event of failures. The value must be one of the following: DO_NOTHING, ROLLBACK, or DELETE.

Hence, the correct answer is: By default, the CloudFormation will roll back the stack.

The option that says: By default, the CloudFormation will complete the stack creation since the EC2 and Amazon EBS resources were already launched is incorrect because the RDS instance creation failed; the CloudFormation service will not consider the stack creation as complete and will roll back any changes made up until that point.

The option that says: By default, the CloudFormation will roll back and delete the stack is incorrect because it will simply roll back the stack by default if it encounters an error during the resource creation process. The resources created are only deleted and not the stack. This is to ensure that the stack remains consistent and that no partial resources are left behind.

The option that says: By default, there will be a Wait Condition that will pause the stack creation and prompt the user to acknowledge the failure is incorrect because the RDS instance creation has already failed, and there is no reason to pause the stack creation process.

questions.question:
An online brokerage firm is using various cloud services in AWS for its mobile and web stock trading application. To meet the IT security compliance requirement, you are tasked to ensure that all API calls made on each of their AWS resources are properly logged.

How can you meet the specified requirement in this scenario?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service logs all API calls made across AWS resources for compliance purposes?
choices: Use Amazon Detective
Use AWS IAM Access Analyzer
Use Amazon VPC Traffic Mirroring
Use CloudTrail
correctAnswers: Use CloudTrail
questions.explanation:
AWS CloudTrail is a service that provides a record of actions taken by a user, role, or an AWS service. CloudTrail captures API calls as events. For an ongoing record of events in your AWS account, you create a trail. A trail enables CloudTrail to deliver log files of events to an Amazon S3 bucket.

You can take advantage of Amazon S3’s bucket notification feature and direct Amazon S3 to publish object-created events to AWS Lambda. Whenever CloudTrail writes logs to your S3 bucket, Amazon S3 can then invoke your Lambda function by passing the Amazon S3 object-created event as a parameter. The S3 event provides information, including the bucket name and key name of the log object that CloudTrail created. Your Lambda function code can read the log object and process the access records logged by CloudTrail. For example, you might write Lambda function code to notify you if a specific API call was made in your account.

Hence, the correct answer is: Use CloudTrail

Using Amazon VPC Traffic Mirroring is incorrect because this is just one of the features of Amazon VPC that allows you to copy network traffic from the Elastic Network Interface (ENI) of your EC2 instances. This is not capable of logging the API calls made to your AWS resources, unlike AWS CloudTrail.

Use Amazon Detective is incorrect because this service simply collects the log data from your AWS resources automatically, and does not track the API calls. Amazon Detective is primarily used to analyze, investigate, and quickly identify the root cause of potential security issues or suspicious activities in your AWS infrastructure.

Using AWS IAM Access Analyzer is incorrect because this service only identifies the resources in your organization and accounts that are shared with an external entity. This is commonly used in identifying the unintended access to your resources and data. For tracking API calls to your AWS resources, you should use CloudTrail instead.

questions.question:
AWS regularly releases service updates and changes to constantly improve its entire global architecture that might affect cloud resources. The organization also uses PutEvents to send internal application events to an event bus for custom monitoring. To ensure the continuous flow of business operations, the organization must be immediately notified of any outages or underlying infrastructure changes introduced by AWS.

Which of the following can help in this scenario?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service notifies you of AWS outages and infrastructure changes affecting your resources?
choices: AWS Billing Dashboard
AWS Trusted Advisor
AWS IAM
AWS Health Dashboard
correctAnswers: AWS Health Dashboard
questions.explanation:
The AWS Health Dashboard is the single place to learn about the availability and operations of AWS services. You can view the overall status of AWS services, and you can sign in to view personalized communications about your particular AWS account or organization. Your account view provides deeper visibility into resource issues, upcoming changes, and important notifications.

AWS Health Dashboard

Upon logging in to the AWS Health Dashboard, you can have a customized overview of the AWS service status that your application relies on. The dashboard can be used to gain insight into particular operational incidents that impact your AWS account. For instance, if you encounter an event regarding a missing Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) volume that is linked to an Amazon EC2 instance, you can swiftly assess the extent of the impact on your resources. This, in turn, can assist in the identification and resolution of the issue.

Hence, the correct answer is: AWS Health Dashboard.

AWS Trusted Advisor is incorrect because it is an online resource that helps you reduce cost, increase performance, and improve security by optimizing your AWS environment, and it also provides real-time guidance to help you provision your resources while following AWS best practices. It does not, however, tell you if there are events that occur with AWS that will affect your business.

AWS IAM is incorrect because it is a web service that typically helps secure access to AWS resources. You use IAM to control who is authenticated and authorized to use resources. IAM does not tell you if something occurs to AWS that will impact your business.

AWS Billing Dashboard is incorrect since it is primarily used to monitor your AWS usage and budget your costs. It shows all relevant information regarding your billing in AWS and does not provide alerts and remediation guidance when AWS is experiencing events that may impact you.

questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer configured Amazon CloudWatch monitoring on an On-Demand, Amazon EBS-backed Amazon EC2 instance, which is deployed in the ap-southeast-1 region.

Which of the following metrics will always show a value of 0?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which CloudWatch metric always shows zero on an EBS-backed EC2 instance with no instance store?
choices: NetworkIn
CPUUtilization
DiskReadOps
NetworkOut
correctAnswers: DiskReadOps
questions.explanation:
DiskReadOps is the metric that counts the completed read operations from all instance store volumes available to the instance in a specified period of time. To calculate the average I/O operations per second (IOPS) for the period, divide the total operations in the period by the number of seconds in that period.

CloudWatch Metrics

If there are no instance store volumes, either the value is 0 or the metric is not reported. The same behavior also applies to DiskWriteOps, DiskReadBytes, and DiskWriteBytes.

Hence, the correct answer is: DiskReadOps.

NetworkIn is incorrect because this metric typically measures the number of bytes received on all network interfaces by the instance. Since any inbound traffic (SSH, HTTP requests, etc.) will generate values, it will not always be 0.

NetworkOut is incorrect because this metric primarily measures the number of bytes sent out from the instance. Outbound traffic (responses, uploads, etc.) will cause non-zero values, so it will not always be 0.

CPUUtilization is incorrect because this metric reflects the percentage of allocated EC2 compute units used. Even idle instances may show small fluctuations, and under load, they will rise significantly. It is not fixed at 0.

questions.question:
Due to the recent IT audit, a financial company was instructed to present the information on AWS environment compliance for Payment Card Industry (PCI) workloads. This is done to provide a better understanding of what specific areas of the PCI standards fall under the company’s responsibility in running their e-commerce website.

Which of the following AWS services should you use in order to provide the necessary information?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service provides on-demand access to PCI compliance reports and documentation?
choices: AWS Certificate Manager
Amazon Macie
AWS Artifact
AWS Trusted Advisor
correctAnswers: AWS Artifact
questions.explanation:
AWS Artifact is your go-to central resource for compliance-related information that matters to you. It provides on-demand access to AWS’ security and compliance reports and select online agreements. Reports available in AWS Artifact include our Service Organization Control (SOC) reports, Payment Card Industry (PCI) reports, and certifications from accreditation bodies across geographies and compliance verticals that validate the implementation and operating effectiveness of AWS security controls. Agreements available in AWS Artifact include the Business Associate Addendum (BAA) and the Nondisclosure Agreement (NDA).

The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) is a proprietary information security standard administered by the PCI Security Standards Council, which was founded by American Express, Discover Financial Services, JCB International, MasterCard Worldwide and Visa Inc.

PCI DSS applies to all entities that store, process or transmit cardholder data (CHD) or sensitive authentication data (SAD), including merchants, processors, acquirers, issuers, and service providers. The PCI DSS is mandated by the card brands and administered by the Payment Card Industry Security Standards Council.

The PCI DSS Attestation of Compliance (AOC) and Responsibility Summary is available to customers by using AWS Artifact, a self-service portal for on-demand access to AWS compliance reports.

Hence, the correct answer is AWS Artifact.

Amazon Macie is incorrect because it is just a security service that uses machine learning to automatically discover, classify, and protect sensitive data in AWS.

AWS Certificate Manager is incorrect because this is a service that simply lets you easily provision, manage, and deploy public and private Secure Sockets Layer/Transport Layer Security (SSL/TLS) certificates for use with AWS services and your internal connected resources. This service does not store certifications or compliance-related documents.

AWS Trusted Advisor is incorrect because this is just an online tool that provides you with real-time guidance to help you provision your resources following AWS best practices. It inspects your AWS environment and makes recommendations for saving money, improving system performance and reliability, or closing security gaps.

questions.question:
A startup launched an online transaction processing application that uses a fleet of EC2 instances deployed across multiple Availability Zones with an Application Load Balancer to evenly distribute the incoming traffic. The CloudOps Engineer provisioned an RDS instance in a private subnet for the application’s database-tier, with a NAT gateway for routing traffic from the database server to the Internet.

Which of the following options can the Engineer implement to increase the scalability and availability of the application? (Select TWO.)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which two changes improve scalability and availability for an EC2 and RDS application architecture?
choices: Switch from a NAT gateway to NAT instance for higher bandwidth and availability
Replace the Application Load Balancer with Route 53
Use Auto Scaling
Configure the RDS instance to use Multi-AZ deployments
correctAnswers: Use Auto Scaling
Configure the RDS instance to use Multi-AZ deployments
questions.explanation:
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling helps you ensure that you have the correct number of Amazon EC2 instances available to handle the load for your application. You create collections of EC2 instances called Auto Scaling groups. You can specify the minimum number of instances in each Auto Scaling group, and Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling ensures that your group never goes below this size. You can specify the maximum number of instances in each Auto Scaling group, and Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling ensures that your group never goes above this size.

Amazon RDS Multi-AZ deployments provide enhanced availability and durability for Database (DB) Instances, making them a natural fit for production database workloads. When you provision a Multi-AZ DB Instance, Amazon RDS automatically creates a primary DB Instance and synchronously replicates the data to a standby instance in a different Availability Zone (AZ).

Hence, the correct answers are:

– Use Auto Scaling.

– Configure the RDS instance to use Multi-AZ deployments.

The option that says: Switch from a NAT gateway to NAT instance for higher bandwidth and availability is incorrect because a NAT gateway is actually much better than a NAT instance in terms of scalability and availability.

The option that says: Replace the Application Load Balancer with Route 53 is incorrect because an application load balancer provides better performance, compared to Route53, in evenly distributing the incoming traffic to the EC2 instances.

questions.question:
A company plans to migrate a legacy web application to AWS Cloud. All the networking and security components are already in place, and now it is necessary to design the web application to be scalable. It would be too costly to redesign the database schema to a NoSQL database; hence, it maintains a relational database structure. Employees also frequently use the web application to access customer information. The monitoring plan includes tracking ALBRequestCountPerTarget to ensure even distribution of traffic across the web servers.

What resources should be provisioned to set up a scalable web application?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What AWS resources provide a scalable architecture for a relational database-backed web application?
choices: Amazon EC2 servers with an ELB in front deployed in multiple regions, and an Amazon RDS instance with a read replica.
Amazon EC2 servers grouped into an auto scaling group with an ELB in front, and an Amazon RDS instance with a read replica.
Amazon EC2 servers with an ELB in front deployed in multiple regions, and an Amazon RDS instance with Multi-AZ deployment.
Amazon EC2 servers grouped into an auto scaling group with an ELB in front, and a Redis cluster.
correctAnswers: Amazon EC2 servers grouped into an auto scaling group with an ELB in front, and an Amazon RDS instance with a read replica.
questions.explanation:
The primary focus here is to develop a scalable web application in AWS. Having an ELB for your EC2 servers will allow you to distribute the load among them. Auto Scaling will scale the number of running instances based on the parameters you’ve set. For the RDS, since we’ll be expecting a lot of read statements, having a read replica set up will help manage the load.

AWS diagram of an Elastic Load Balancer routing user traffic to instances across two Availability Zones.

Hence, the correct answer is: Amazon EC2 servers grouped into an auto scaling group with an ELB in front, and an Amazon RDS instance with a read replica.

The option that says: Amazon EC2 servers with an ELB in front deployed in multiple regions, and an Amazon RDS instance with a read replica is incorrect because deployment in multiple regions is for the sake of high availability, which is not what the question is asking for. In addition, an ELB can only route traffic in one specific AWS region.

The option that says: Amazon EC2 servers grouped into an auto scaling group with an ELB in front, and a Redis cluster is incorrect because Redis is primarily used for in-memory caching and not as a relational database.

The option that says: Amazon EC2 servers with an ELB in front deployed in multiple regions, and an Amazon RDS instance with Multi-AZ deployment is incorrect. It should include Auto Scaling to meet the scalability requirement.

questions.question:
A company has a development and operations team that handles software and infrastructure management. The CloudOps Engineer discovered that some developers are using individual AWS accounts that are not under the direct control of the company. To centrally manage and govern the AWS environment, the operations team requested to link the individual accounts to the company’s central organization via the AWS Organizations service.

Which of the following options fulfills this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you add existing individual AWS accounts to a central AWS Organizations organization?
choices: Create a service control policy to allow each AWS account to join the central organization.
Create an organization on each existing AWS account and link it to the central organization.
Use AWS IAM to add existing AWS accounts to the central organization.
Send invitations to existing AWS accounts using from the central organization using the AWS Organizations console.
correctAnswers: Send invitations to existing AWS accounts using from the central organization using the AWS Organizations console.
questions.explanation:
AWS Organizations helps you centrally manage and govern your environment as you grow and scale your AWS resources. Using AWS Organizations, you can programmatically create new AWS accounts and allocate resources, group accounts to organize your workflows, apply policies to accounts or groups for governance, and simplify billing by using a single payment method for all of your accounts.

In addition, AWS Organizations is integrated with other AWS services so you can define central configurations, security mechanisms, audit requirements, and resource sharing across accounts in your organization. AWS Organizations is available to all AWS customers at no additional charge.

AWS Organizations console allows you to manage invitations that you send to other AWS accounts. When sending an invitation, you can only do it using the organization’s management account. Take note that an AWS account can only join one organization.

Based on the given scenario, you can use AWS Organizations to invite individual AWS accounts to join the company’s organization. Remember that the account must accept the invitation first before it is added to the organization.

Hence, the correct answer is: Send invitations to existing AWS accounts using from the central organization using the AWS Organizations console.

The option that says: Create an organization on each existing AWS account and link it to the central organization is incorrect because if each AWS account created an organization, you won’t be able to invite those accounts to join the company’s organization.

The option that says: Use AWS IAM to add existing AWS accounts to the central organization is incorrect because you should use AWS Organizations to send an invite to existing AWS accounts. Using an AWS IAM alone is not enough to connect the AWS accounts.

The option that says: Create a service control policy to allow each AWS account to join the central organization is incorrect because this option only allows you to manage the permissions of the underlying AWS accounts in your organization. Take note that you need to send an invite to each AWS account using the management account to join the company’s AWS Organizations.

questions.question:
A large enterprise has hundreds of AWS accounts that are grouped in several organizational units (OU) using AWS Organizations. Web Identity federation is being used to grant the necessary AWS permissions, create resources, and authenticate employees. For reporting purposes, the SysOps Administrator must determine and track the employees who issued requests to AWS Organizations for new AWS accounts.

What is the MOST suitable solution that the Administrator should do to satisfy this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which service tracks AWS API calls made by federated users authenticating via Web Identity?
choices: Use AWS CloudTrail to track the calls made by the federated users.
Use AWS IAM Access Advisor to track the calls made by the federated users.
Set up AWS IAM Identity Center to track the calls made by the federated users.
Set up and track the user logs in the identity provider (IdP) to track the calls made by the federated users.
correctAnswers: Use AWS CloudTrail to track the calls made by the federated users.
questions.explanation:
IAM and AWS STS are integrated with AWS CloudTrail, a service that provides a record of actions taken by an IAM user or role. CloudTrail captures all API calls for IAM and AWS STS as events, including calls from the console and from API calls. If you create a trail, you can enable the continuous delivery of CloudTrail events to an Amazon S3 bucket. If you don’t configure a trail, you can still view the most recent events in the CloudTrail console in Event history. You can use CloudTrail to get information about the request that was made to IAM or AWS STS.

CloudTrail logs all authenticated API requests (made with credentials) to IAM and AWS STS API operations. CloudTrail also logs unauthenticated requests to the AWS STS actions, AssumeRoleWithSAML and AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity, and logs information provided by the identity provider. You can use this information to map calls made by a federated user with an assumed role back to the originating external federated caller. In the case of AssumeRole, you can map calls back to the originating AWS service or to the account of the originating user. The userIdentity section of the JSON data in the CloudTrail log entry contains the information that you need to map the AssumeRole* request with a specific federated user.

Hence, the correct answer is: Use AWS CloudTrail to track the calls made by the federated users.

The option that says: Use AWS IAM Access Advisor to track the calls made by the federated users is incorrect because the IAM Access Advisor can’t directly track all the calls made by the federated users. It usually provides the last timestamp when an IAM entity (e.g., user, role, or group) accessed an AWS service. Moreover, the IAM Access Advisor only provides limited information, unlike AWS CloudTrail.

The option that says: Set up and track the user logs in the identity provider (IdP) to track the calls made by the federated users is incorrect. Although this solution is feasible, it entails a lot of custom work to track each and every federated request to AWS. A more suitable solution is to use AWS CloudTrail.

The option that says: Set up AWS IAM Identity Center to track the calls made by the federated users is incorrect because AWS IAM Identity Center simply provides users with single sign-on access to all their assigned accounts and applications from one place. You can’t use this service to track the activity of your federated users.

questions.question:
A social media startup is developing an application that allows users to send pictures, videos, and messages to their contacts. The users should have the ability to log in to their profile using any of their social media accounts. A serverless backend that is composed of several AWS Lambda functions will store the user details on an Amazon Aurora Serverless database. The solution must reduce the failover times by automatically connecting to a new database instance while preserving application connections.

Which combination of the following steps should you implement to satisfy the given requirement? (Select TWO.)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which two services handle social media authentication and reduce Aurora database failover time for Lambda?
choices: Launch an Amazon RDS Proxy in between the serverless application and Amazon Aurora Serverless database.
Use Amazon Cognito for user authentication with an appropriate IAM Role.
Enable the Performance Insights feature in the Amazon Aurora Serverless database which will automatically connect to a new Aurora database instance while preserving application connections.
Integrate the serverless application with AWS Security Hub to allow social media authentication and user management.
Enable the AWS IAM Identity Center in your AWS account with a SAML 2.0 IdP identity source to handle user authentication.
correctAnswers: Use Amazon Cognito for user authentication with an appropriate IAM Role.
Launch an Amazon RDS Proxy in between the serverless application and Amazon Aurora Serverless database.
questions.explanation:
Amazon Cognito identity pools assign your authenticated users a set of temporary, limited privilege credentials to access your AWS resources. The permissions for each user are controlled through IAM roles that you create. You can define rules to choose the role for each user based on claims in the user’s ID token. You can define a default role for authenticated users. You can also define a separate IAM role with limited permissions for guest users who are not authenticated.

Using Amazon RDS Proxy allows your applications to pool and share database connections to improve their ability to scale. RDS Proxy makes applications more resilient to database failures by automatically connecting to a standby DB instance while preserving application connections. By using RDS Proxy, you can also enforce AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) authentication for databases and securely store credentials in AWS Secrets Manager.

Using RDS Proxy, you can handle unpredictable surges in database traffic that otherwise might cause issues due to oversubscribing connections or creating new connections at a fast rate. RDS Proxy establishes a database connection pool and reuses connections in this pool without the memory and CPU overhead of opening a new database connection each time. To protect the database against oversubscription, you can control the number of database connections that are created.

Hence, the correct answers are:

– Use Amazon Cognito for user authentication with an appropriate IAM Role.

– Launch an Amazon RDS Proxy in between the serverless application and Amazon Aurora Serverless database.

The option that says: Enable the Performance Insights feature in the Amazon Aurora Serverless database which will automatically connect to a new Aurora database instance while preserving application connections is incorrect because Performance Insights only expands the existing Amazon RDS monitoring features to illustrate and help you analyze your database performance. This particular feature is not capable of automatically connecting to a new Aurora database instance while preserving application connections. You have to use an Amazon RDS Proxy to meet this requirement.

The option that says: Integrate the serverless application with AWS Security Hub to allow social media authentication and user management is incorrect because the AWS Security Hub service is not used for user authentication. AWS Security Hub just provides you with a comprehensive view of your security state in AWS and helps you check your environment against security industry standards and best practices.

The option that says: Enable the AWS IAM Identity Center in your AWS account with a SAML 2.0 IdP identity source to handle user authentication is incorrect because AWS IAM Identity Center is not a suitable service for handling user authentication using their social media accounts. It is primarily designed for managing access to multiple AWS accounts and integrating with identity providers through SAML 2.0. It does not have native support for the OAuth-based authentication typically required for social media account authentication.

questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer sends a ping command on a home computer, which has an IP address of 110.237.99.166, to an EC2 instance that has a private IP address of 172.31.17.140. However, the response ping is dropped and does not reach the home computer. To troubleshoot the issue, the Engineer checked the flow logs of the VPC and saw the following entries as shown below.

2 123456789010 eni-1235b8ca 110.237.99.166 172.31.17.140 0 0 1 4 336 1432917027 1432917142 ACCEPT OK 2 123456789010 eni-1235b8ca 172.31.17.140 110.237.99.166 0 0 1 4 336 1432917094 1432917142 REJECT OK

Which is the most likely root cause of this issue?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Why does a VPC Flow Log show ACCEPT for inbound ping but REJECT for the outbound reply?
choices: Network ACL does not permit inbound ICMP traffic.
Network ACL permits inbound ICMP traffic but does not permit outbound ICMP traffic.
Security group has an inbound rule that allows ICMP traffic but does not have an outbound rule to explicitly allow outgoing ICMP traffic.
Security group's inbound rules do not allow ICMP traffic.
correctAnswers: Network ACL permits inbound ICMP traffic but does not permit outbound ICMP traffic.
questions.explanation:
In this scenario, the ping command from your home computer to your EC2 instance failed, and there are two VPC Flow logs provided in the scenario. The logs basically mean that the first one is the record of the traffic flow that goes from your home computer to your EC2 instance, and the latter is the record of the traffic flow that goes back from the EC2 instance back to your home computer.

Apparently, the first one is an ACCEPT record, and the second one is a REJECT record, which means that the incoming traffic was successfully accepted by your EC2 instance, but the response, or the outgoing traffic, was rejected by either your security group or network ACL.

If you’re using flow logs to diagnose overly restrictive or permissive security group rules or network ACL rules, then be aware of the statefulness of these resources. Security groups are stateful — this means that responses to allowed traffic are also allowed, even if the rules in your security group do not permit it. Conversely, network ACLs are stateless; therefore, responses to allowed traffic are subject to network ACL rules.

For example, you use the ping command from your home computer (IP address is 203.0.113.12) to your instance (the network interface’s private IP address is 172.31.16.139). Your security group’s inbound rules allow ICMP traffic, and the outbound rules do not allow ICMP traffic; however, because security groups are stateful, the response ping from your instance is allowed. Your network ACL permits inbound ICMP traffic but does not permit outbound ICMP traffic. Because network ACLs are stateless, the response ping is dropped and does not reach your home computer.

In a flow log, this is displayed as two flow log records:

An ACCEPT record for the originating ping that was allowed by both the network ACL and the security group and therefore was allowed to reach your instance.

A REJECT record for the response ping that the network ACL denied.

2 123456789010 eni-1235b8ca 203.0.113.12 172.31.16.139 0 0 1 4 336 1432917027 1432917142 ACCEPT OK

2 123456789010 eni-1235b8ca 172.31.16.139 203.0.113.12 0 0 1 4 336 1432917094 1432917142 REJECT OK

A flow log record is a space-separated string that has the following format:

<version> <account-id> <interface-id> <srcaddr> <dstaddr> <srcport> <dstport> <protocol> <packets> <bytes> <start> <end> <action> <log-status> Hence, the correct answer is: Network ACL permits inbound ICMP traffic but does not permit outbound ICMP traffic.

The option that says: Security group has an inbound rule that allow ICMP traffic but does not have an outbound rule to explicitly allow outgoing ICMP traffic is incorrect because security groups are stateful. Hence, the response ping from your EC2 instance will still be allowed without explicitly allowing outgoing ICMP traffic.

The options that say: Security group’s inbound rules do not allow ICMP traffic and Network ACL does not permit inbound ICMP traffic are both incorrect because the first flow log clearly shows that the incoming traffic was successfully accepted by your EC2 instance, which is why the issue lies on the outgoing traffic. The second flow log shows that the response, or the outgoing traffic, was rejected by either your security group or network ACL.

questions.question:
An organization has created block storage volumes using AWS Storage Gateway and mounted them as iSCSI devices to its application servers on-premises. After three months, the organization noticed a performance degradation of its iSCSI devices. The CloudOps Engineer has been tasked to check the metrics of each resource. Upon investigation, the Administrator saw that the CacheHitPercent is below 50%, and the CachePercentUsed is above 80%.

Which of the following options should the Engineer do to fix this problem?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you fix Storage Gateway performance when CacheHitPercent is low and CachePercentUsed is high?
choices: Launch a new disk with a larger capacity for the cached volume host. Use the AWS Management Console to edit the local disk and configure the new disk as the cached volume.
Create a snapshot of your volumes and use it to create new volumes.
Use a larger block size to improve the performance of your tape drives.
Implement a RAID 1 configuration in your on-premises environment.
correctAnswers: Launch a new disk with a larger capacity for the cached volume host. Use the AWS Management Console to edit the local disk and configure the new disk as the cached volume.
questions.explanation:
Volume Gateway presents cloud-backed iSCSI block storage volumes to your on-premises applications. Volume Gateway stores and manages on-premises data in Amazon S3 on your behalf and operates in either cache mode or stored mode. In the cached Volume Gateway mode, your primary data is stored in Amazon S3 while retaining your frequently accessed data locally in the cache for low latency access. In the stored Volume Gateway mode, your primary data is stored locally. And your entire dataset is available for low latency access on-premises while also asynchronously getting backed up to Amazon S3.

How Volume Gateway works

When adding a cache or upload buffer to an existing gateway, it is important to create new disks in your host (hypervisor or Amazon EC2 instance). Don’t change the size of existing disks if the disks have been previously allocated as either a cache or upload buffer. Do not remove cache disks that have been allocated as cache storage.

Based on the given scenario, the organization is using a Volume Gateway since the iSCSI protocol is used. The specific type of Volume Gateway being used is a Cached Volume variant because the metrics CacheHitPercent and CachePercentUsed are not supported in Stored Volumes. To increase the gateway’s upload buffer or cache storage capacity, you must create a new disk in your host and use the AWS Management Console to edit the local disk, and select the new disk as the cached volume.

Hence, the correct answer is: Launch a new disk with a larger capacity for the cached volume host. Use the AWS Management Console to edit the local disk and configure the new disk as the cached volume.

The option that says: Create a snapshot of your volumes and use it to create new volumes is incorrect because snapshots will affect the CacheHitPercent and CachePercentUsed metrics in Storage Gateway. You must create a larger disk and select it as the cached volume to rectify this issue.

The option that says: Implement a RAID 1 configuration in your on-premises environment is incorrect because RAID 1 will not increase your disks’ performance. It only replicates the data to at least two disks and produces near real-time failover as required by mission-critical applications.

The option that says: Use a larger block size to improve the performance of your tape drives is incorrect. Remember that the organization is using a Volume Gateway and not a Tape Gateway. To resolve the performance problem, you should create a new disk in your host and select the disk as the cached volume.

questions.question:
A web application is running on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB) within an Auto Scaling group. ALB access logs reveal that the application is being targeted by malicious traffic originating from a single public IP address. The Cloud engineer must implement a solution that will block this IP address.

Which solution must the Cloud engineer implement to meet this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you block a specific malicious IP address from reaching an Application Load Balancer?
choices: Utilize AWS Resource Access Manager (AWS RAM) to administer traffic rules and block malicious activity from the internet. Associate the ALB with AWS RAM.
Create an NACL rule to deny all inbound traffic from the public IP address. Associate the ALB with the NACL.
Create an IP set that includes the public IP address. Configure a web ACL with a rule set to BLOCK the IP set in AWS WAF. Associate the ALB with the web ACL.
Utilize Amazon Detective to prevent malicious activity from the internet and monitor the traffic. Associate the ALB with Amazon Detective.
correctAnswers: Create an IP set that includes the public IP address. Configure a web ACL with a rule set to BLOCK the IP set in AWS WAF. Associate the ALB with the web ACL.
questions.explanation:
AWS WAF is a web application firewall that lets you monitor the HTTP(S) requests that are forwarded to your protected web application resources. You can protect the following resource types:

– Amazon CloudFront distribution

– Amazon API Gateway REST API

– Application Load Balancer

– AWS AppSync GraphQL API

– Amazon Cognito user pool

– AWS App Runner service

– AWS Verified Access instance

AWS WAF lets you control access to your content. Based on criteria that you specify, such as the IP addresses that requests originate from or the values of query strings, the service associated with your protected resource responds to requests either with the requested content, with an HTTP 403 status code (Forbidden), or with a custom response.

A web access control list (web ACL) gives you fine-grained control over all of the HTTP(S) web requests that your protected resource responds to.

To create a new web ACL, use the web ACL creation wizard.

You can use criteria like the following to allow or block requests:

– IP address origin of the request

– Country of origin of the request

– String match or regular expression (regex) match in a part of the request

– Size of a particular part of the request

-Detection of malicious SQL code or scripting

You can utilize web ACL to allow or block requests from a specific IP address. You can create an IP set that will include the IP address and use a rule that will block the IP set when configuring a web ACL.

Hence, the correct answer is: Create an IP set that includes the public IP address. Configure a web ACL with a rule set to BLOCK the IP set in AWS WAF. Associate the ALB with the web ACL.

The option that says: Utilize AWS Resource Access Manager (AWS RAM) to administer traffic rules and block malicious activity from the internet. Associate the ALB with AWS RAM is incorrect because AWS RAM is primarily used to securely share your resources across AWS accounts, within your organization or organizational units (OUs), and with AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) roles and users for supported resource types, it is not for blocking traffic from a specific IP address. The AWS RAM only supports a limited number of resource types, and resources for an Application Load Balancer is not supported. It is an entirely different service and is impossible to utilize for this approach.

The option that says: Utilize Amazon Detective to prevent malicious activity from the internet and monitor the traffic. Associate the ALB with Amazon Detective is incorrect. Although Amazon Detective can help investigate and analyze IP traffic to identify malicious activities, it is still inadequate as Amazon Detective is not capable of blocking requests from a specific IP address. With Amazon Detective, you can analyze and visualize security data to investigate potential security issues. Detective collects and analyzes events that describe IP traffic, AWS management operations, and malicious or unauthorized activity from AWS CloudTrail logs, VPC Flow Logs, Amazon GuardDuty findings, and, Amazon EKS audit logs.

The option that says: Create an NACL rule to deny all inbound traffic from the public IP address. Associate the ALB with the NACL is incorrect. Network Access Control Lists (NACLs) can only be associated with subnets, not directly with individual resources like an ALB. Moreover, managing IP blocklists with NACLs can be less flexible compared to AWS WAF.

questions.question:
A company is using Amazon S3 to serve static content and Amazon CloudFront to speed up content delivery to its users across the globe. For the next business cycle, the company plans on improving these services to attract more customers and provide a better user experience. To plan the next step, the CloudOps Engineer requires more information regarding the activities occurring in the AWS resources. CloudFront includes a variety of reports that the Engineer can use to view usage and activity within the CloudFront distributions.

How can the Engineer utilize these reports for this purpose? (Select TWO.)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which two CloudFront reports provide data on frequently accessed objects and HTTP request counts?
choices: Use Top Referrers Reports to get statistics on viewer requests grouped by HTTP status code.
Use the Popular Objects Report to determine what objects are frequently being accessed, and get statistics on those objects.
Use Usage Reports to learn about the different types of browsers that your users frequently use to access your content.
Use Usage Reports to know the number of HTTP and HTTPS requests that CloudFront responds to from edge locations in selected regions.
Use Cache Statistics Reports to display a list of the 25 website domains that originated the most HTTP and HTTPS requests for objects that CloudFront is distributing for a specified distribution.
correctAnswers: Use the Popular Objects Report to determine what objects are frequently being accessed, and get statistics on those objects.
Use Usage Reports to know the number of HTTP and HTTPS requests that CloudFront responds to from edge locations in selected regions.
questions.explanation:
Amazon CloudFront is a web service that gives businesses and web application developers an easy and cost-effective way to distribute content with low latency and high data transfer speeds. Like other AWS services, Amazon CloudFront is a self-service, pay-per-use offering requiring no long-term commitments or minimum fees. With CloudFront, your files are delivered to end-users using a global network of edge locations.

Amazon CloudFront

The statements mentioned above are correct because you are using the correct report for each purpose. The Popular Objects Report can determine what objects are frequently being accessed and get statistics on those objects. Usage Reports tells you the number of HTTP and HTTPS requests that CloudFront responds to from edge locations in selected regions. Viewers Reports can determine the locations of the viewers that access your content most frequently.

Hence, the correct answers are:

– Use the Popular Objects Report to determine what objects are frequently being accessed, and get statistics on those objects.

– Use Usage Reports to know the number of HTTP and HTTPS requests that CloudFront responds to from edge locations in selected regions.

The option that says: Use Cache Statistics Reports to display a list of the 25 website domains that originated the most HTTP and HTTPS requests for objects that CloudFront is distributing for a specified distribution is incorrect because this is typically provided by the Top Referrers Reports and not the Cache Statistics Reports.

The option that says: Use Top Referrers Reports to get statistics on viewer requests grouped by HTTP status code is incorrect because this is primarily provided by the Cache Statistics Reports and not the Top Referrers Reports.

The option that says: Use Usage Reports to learn about the different types of browsers that your users frequently use to access your content is incorrect because this is actually provided by the Viewers Reports and not the Usage Reports.

questions.question:
A NodeJS application uses an S3 bucket to store user-generated data. The bucket policy should always be monitored to secure the data of the users and verify that the files are not publicly accessible over the Internet. An S3 Lifecycle policy was also configured to transition older objects to colder storage tiers for cost optimization. The CloudOps Engineer needs an automated way to assess resource configurations and detect configuration changes across the AWS environment to support continuous compliance and self-governance.

Which of the following is the best approach to satisfy this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service automates assessment of resource configurations and detects configuration changes?
choices: Use AWS Config.
Configure the bucket policy to deny all read and write actions from unauthorized users.
Configure the bucket policy of the S3 bucket using IAM to prevent any unauthorized access.
Enable MFA Delete and Versioning in the S3 bucket.
correctAnswers: Use AWS Config.
questions.explanation:
AWS Config enables continuous monitoring of your AWS resources, making it simple to assess, audit, and record resource configurations and changes. AWS Config does this through the use of rules that define the desired configuration state of your AWS resources.
