The option that says: AWS does not currently have enough available On-Demand capacity to service your request is incorrect because this is the root cause when you get the InsufficientInstanceCapacity error when you try to launch a new instance or restart a stopped instance, but it does not cause the instance to be in the terminated state immediately after restarting it.

questions.question: A tech company uses AWS Organizations to manage multiple AWS accounts. The CloudOps Engineer wants to ensure that all security groups attached to Amazon EC2 instances do not allow SSH traffic. The engineer needs to aggregate the configuration data from different AWS accounts in multiple AWS regions. The engineer also plans to use the AWS-DisableIncomingSSHOnPort22 Automation runbook to automatically remediate any security group that violates the SSH restriction. What should the CloudOps Engineer do to achieve the requirements?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you aggregate and enforce security group compliance across multiple AWS accounts using Config?
choices: Create an AWS Config aggregator resource for each resource in the organization. Create config rules on each AWS account to monitor security groups.
Create a custom config rule to monitor changes to the security group in all EC2 instances provisioned in the management account.
Create an AWS Config aggregator resource for all AWS accounts in the organization. Enable Config and create a config rule on all accounts to monitor security groups.
Create a managed config rule to monitor all security groups when they don't comply with the rules.
correctAnswers: Create an AWS Config aggregator resource for all AWS accounts in the organization. Enable Config and create a config rule on all accounts to monitor security groups.
questions.explanation:
An aggregator is an AWS Config resource type that collects AWS Config configuration and compliance data from the following:

– Multiple accounts and multiple regions.

– Single account and multiple regions.

– An organization in AWS Organizations and all the accounts in that organization that have AWS Config enabled.

Use an aggregator to view the resource configuration and compliance data recorded in AWS Config.

AWS Config Aggregator

On the Aggregator page, you can do the following:

– Create an aggregator by specifying the source account IDs or organization and regions from which you want to aggregate data.

– Edit and delete an aggregator.

Hence, the correct answer is: Create an AWS Config aggregator resource for all AWS accounts in the organization. Enable Config and create a config rule on all accounts to monitor security groups.

The option that says: Create an AWS Config aggregator resource for each resource in the organization. Create config rules on each AWS account to monitor security groups is incorrect because you can’t create an aggregator for each AWS resource. You only need to specify the source account ID and the region when creating an aggregator, not the specific resources that need to be monitored.

The option that says: Create a custom config rule to monitor changes to the security group in all EC2 instances provisioned in the management account is incorrect. A custom config rule will not be enough to collect compliance status on multiple AWS accounts. You need to set up an aggregator.

The option that says: Create a managed config rule to monitor all security groups when they don’t comply with the rules is incorrect because a managed config rule only monitors resources in a single AWS account. You need to collect compliance information about resources in other AWS accounts too, so this option does not meet the requirement.

questions.question:
A company is hosting a multi-tiered web application that consists of an e-commerce module as well as a blogging site that fetches data from a database. Some articles, which are static web pages, have lots of page hits and sometimes cause the application to behave slowly.

Which of the following can be used to alleviate the issue of slow loading times when many users are visiting the application for such pages, in the MOST cost-effective way?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What is the most cost-effective way to offload high-traffic static web pages from an application server?
choices: Consider hosting the web pages using static web site hosting in S3
Consider placing ElastiCache in front of the database
Consider creating a Read Replica of the database
Consider using the latency policy in Route 53
correctAnswers: Consider hosting the web pages using static web site hosting in S3
questions.explanation:
Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) is an object storage service offering industry-leading scalability, data availability, security, and performance. Customers of all sizes and industries can store and protect any amount of data for virtually any use case, such as data lakes, cloud-native applications, and mobile apps. With cost-effective storage classes and easy-to-use management features, you can optimize costs, organize data, and configure fine-tuned access controls to meet specific business, organizational, and compliance requirements.

You can utilize Amazon S3 to host static web pages. Static pages can also contain client-side scripts. By contrast, a dynamic website relies on server-side processing, including server-side scripts such as PHP, JSP, or ASP.NET. Amazon S3 does not support server-side scripting. In addition, you can also use a CloudFront distribution as a Content Delivery Network for your static contents.

Hence, the correct answer is: Consider hosting the web pages using static web site hosting in S3.

The option that says: Consider placing ElastiCache in front of the database is incorrect because is ElastiCache is used for caching content being frequently fetched from a database.

The option that says: Consider creating a Read Replica of the database is incorrect because Read Replicas are used for scaling read-heavy database workloads in RDS.

The option that says: Consider using the latency policy in Route 53 is incorrect since this is used for routing of traffic between multiple sites.

questions.question:
A data analytics firm has opened a new service center in Africa, and it must be operational in a few days. The new service center decided to contact Company HQ to request a list of running AWS resources and configurations being used in other service centers. The priority is to create an AWS CloudFormation template that includes initial resources and configurations crucial for operations. Remaining templates for less critical resources will follow later. The team previously used ReleaseAddress to manage Elastic IP assignments, but that is not required in the new service center.

Which of the choices should be sent out first?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which CloudFormation template contains the most critical initial resources for a data analytics operation?
choices: CloudFormation design that includes Amazon EC2 servers, Amazon Redshift, and Amazon Athena configurations.
CloudFormation design that includes Amazon EC2 servers, Amazon EBS, AWS Glue protocols, and Amazon ElastiCache configurations.
CloudFormation design that includes Amazon EC2 servers, Amazon CloudWatch custom metrics, and Amazon SQS configurations.
CloudFormation design that includes Amazon EC2 servers, Amazon EBS, Amazon CloudFront settings, and Amazon S3 configurations.
correctAnswers: CloudFormation design that includes Amazon EC2 servers, Amazon Redshift, and Amazon Athena configurations.
questions.explanation:
Given that the company is a data analytics firm, its initial priority should be establishing the core tools required to ingest, store, and query data immediately. Amazon EC2 provides the foundational compute power, Amazon Redshift serves as a robust data warehouse, and Amazon Athena enables instant, serverless SQL querying for ad-hoc analysis.

The 3-step CloudFormation process, from template creation and S3 storage to stack deployment.

Hence, the correct answer is: CloudFormation design that includes Amazon EC2 servers, Amazon Redshift, and Amazon Athena configurations.

The option that says: CloudFormation design that includes Amazon EC2 servers, Amazon EBS, AWS Glue protocols, and Amazon ElastiCache configurations is incorrect because AWS Glue is primarily used for ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes to prepare data for analysis. However, without the foundational data infrastructure like a data warehouse (Redshift) or a serverless query engine (Athena) set up first, the firm lacks the primary tools required to actually analyze that data. Furthermore, ElastiCache is primarily for in-memory caching and fast data retrieval, which isn’t the core requirement for getting an analytics center operational.

The option that says: CloudFormation design that includes Amazon EC2 servers, Amazon EBS, Amazon CloudFront settings, and Amazon S3 configurations is incorrect because CloudFront is a Content Delivery Network (CDN) service typically used for high-speed web content delivery to end users. While S3 is highly relevant for a data lake, a CDN does not address the firm’s need to perform complex analytics.

The option that says: CloudFormation design that includes Amazon EC2 servers, Amazon CloudWatch custom metrics, and Amazon SQS configurations is incorrect. CloudWatch is primarily designed for monitoring your resources, and SQS is a message queuing service for decoupling application components. Neither of these provides the analytics, querying, or warehousing capabilities necessary for the firm’s core business.

questions.question:
A tech startup plans to launch a data analytics application that uses a MongoDB database and an NGINX server. The SysOps Administrator is instructed to provision a highly available storage solution that provides more than 100,000 IOPS in 4kB random I/O reads for the database.

Which of the following options will meet this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which EBS volume type supports more than 100,000 IOPS for random I/O database workloads?
choices: Enable the AutoEnableIO property on a General Purpose SSD (gp3) volume.
Provisioned IOPS SSD (io1) Amazon EBS Volumes
Storage Optimized Instances
Throughput Optimized HDD (st1) Amazon EBS Volumes
correctAnswers: Provisioned IOPS SSD (io1) Amazon EBS Volumes
questions.explanation:
Provisioned IOPS SSD volumes utilize solid-state drives (SSDs) and are best suited for high-performance Amazon EBS storage volumes. They are intended for critical workloads with high IOPS and throughput requirements that demand low latency. These volumes deliver their specified IOPS performance 99.9% of the time. Specifically tailored for I/O-intensive workloads, such as database operations, Provisioned IOPS SSD (io1) volumes ensure consistent and reliable storage performance. When creating the volume, you can specify the desired IOPS rate, and Amazon EBS will maintain the provisioned performance 99.9% of the time. By combining multiple io1 volumes, you can achieve the necessary IOPS for your workload.

EBS Volumes

Hence, the correct answer is: Provisioned IOPS SSD (io1) Amazon EBS Volumes.

The option that says: Storage Optimized Instances is incorrect. While storage-optimized instances are designed for high I/O operations, they rely on instance store volumes that are directly attached to the instance. Data stored on instance store volumes is ephemeral and will be lost if the instance is stopped or terminated.

The option that says: Throughput Optimized HDD (st1) Amazon EBS Volumes is incorrect because this volume is primarily designed for high throughput (sequential I/O) rather than high IOPS (random I/O). It is not suitable for providing the required IOPS and performance for the MongoDB database.

The option that says: Enable the AutoEnableIO property on a General Purpose SSD (gp3) volume is incorrect because the AutoEnableIO property is typically used to automatically re-enable I/O operations on EBS volumes that are placed in a potentially inconsistent state, such as during certain system events or issues. It does not enhance or modify the volume’s IOPS performance. Furthermore, gp3 volumes have a maximum IOPS limit of 16,000, which does not satisfy the requirement of over 100,000 IOPS.

questions.question:
An IT Consulting company has a VPC in the us-east-1 (N. Virginia) region for its business operations. Last month, the entire us-east-1 AWS region went down, which totally rendered all of the cloud systems unavailable and eventually resulted in a financial loss. The engineer also uses synthetics:GetCanaryRuns to retrieve historical run data from Amazon CloudWatch Synthetics canaries. To prevent this from happening again, the CTO instructed the CloudOps Engineer to set up a notification system that provides alerts via a Slack messaging channel when AWS is experiencing events that may impact the cloud resources.

Which of the following options should the Engineer implement to meet this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you send Slack notifications when AWS Health events indicate a regional service disruption?
choices: Use a combination of CloudWatch Alarms, AWS Lambda functions, and Amazon SQS to send a notification to a Slack channel when an event occurs.
Set up an alert using AWS Trusted Advisor to send a notification to a Slack channel when an event occurs.
Use AWS Health Events with Amazon EventBridge and an AWS Lambda function to send a notification to a Slack channel when an event occurs.
Use a combination of CloudWatch Alarms and Amazon SNS to send a notification to a Slack channel when an event occurs.
correctAnswers: Use AWS Health Events with Amazon EventBridge and an AWS Lambda function to send a notification to a Slack channel when an event occurs.
questions.explanation:
You can use Amazon EventBridge to detect and react to changes in the status of AWS Personal Health Dashboard (AWS Health) events. Then, based on the rules that you create, Amazon EventBridge invokes one or more target actions when an event matches the values that you specify in a rule.

AWS Health Events with Amazon EventBridge and a Lambda function to send a notification to a Slack channel Depending on the type of event, you can send notifications, capture event information, take corrective action, initiate events, or take other actions. You can select the following types of targets when using Amazon EventBridge as part of your AWS Health workflow:

– AWS Lambda functions

– Kinesis streams

– Amazon SQS queues

– Built-in targets (CloudWatch alarm actions)

– Amazon SNS topics

The following are some use cases:

– Use a Lambda function to pass a notification to a Slack channel when an event occurs.

– Send custom text or SMS notifications with Amazon SNS when an AWS Health event happens by using Lambda and Amazon EventBridge.

Hence, the correct answer is: Use AWS Health Events with Amazon EventBridge and an AWS Lambda function to send a notification to a Slack channel when an event occurs.

The option that says: Set up an alert using AWS Trusted Advisor to send a notification to a Slack channel when an event occurs is incorrect because AWS Trusted Advisor is an online resource to help you reduce cost, increase performance, and improve security by optimizing your AWS environment; hence, this will not provide you with the needed information for any AWS outages.

The option that says: Use a combination of CloudWatch Alarms and Amazon SNS to send a notification to a Slack channel when an event occurs is incorrect because CloudWatch only monitors the health of the resources that you own based on certain metrics, but it does not check the underlying hardware that hosts the AWS resources.

The option that says: Use a combination of CloudWatch Alarms, AWS Lambda functions, and Amazon SQS to send a notification to a Slack channel when an event occurs is incorrect because CloudWatch cannot detect if AWS is experiencing events that may impact your cloud resources. You have to use AWS Health Events instead. Moreover, Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) is incorrect because this is primarily used as a queue and not for sending notifications. You have to use Amazon SNS instead of Amazon SQS.

questions.question:
A large IT solutions company with 200 technical personnel uses AWS Organizations to manage its multi-account AWS environment. All AWS accounts should be able to read a specific Amazon S3 bucket in the management account. Based on the company policy, the S3 bucket should not be accessible outside the organization. The CloudOps Engineer needs to create a bucket policy to create the necessary permissions.

Which parameters should be specified on the bucket policy to accomplish this task in the MOST efficient approach?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you write an S3 bucket policy that allows access only to all accounts within an AWS Organization?
choices: Manually list all AWS member accounts as the principal.
Declare a (*) wildcard as the principal and set PrincipalOrgId as a condition.
Define PrincipalOrgId as the principal.
Declare the organization's root account ID as the principal.
correctAnswers: Declare a (*) wildcard as the principal and set PrincipalOrgId as a condition.
questions.explanation:
To grant permission to everyone, also referred as anonymous access, you set the wildcard (“*”) as the Principal value. For example, if you configure your bucket as a website, you want all the objects in the bucket to be publicly accessible. The following are equivalent.

– "Principal":"*"

– "Principal":{"AWS":"*"}

aws:PrincipalOrgID

Use this key to compare the identifier of the organization in AWS Organizations to which the requesting principal belongs with the identifier specified in the policy.

Availability – This key is included in the request context only if the principal is a member of an organization.

This global key provides an alternative to listing all the account IDs for all AWS accounts in an organization. You can use this condition key to simplify specifying the Principal element in a resource-based policy. You can specify the organization ID in the condition element. When you add and remove accounts, policies that include the aws:PrincipalOrgID key automatically include the correct accounts and don’t require manual updating.

For example, the following Amazon S3 bucket policy allows members of any account in the o-xxxxxxxxxxx organization to add an object into the policy-ninja-dev bucket.

To efficiently allow all members in the organization to read the S3 bucket, you need to use the (*) wildcard character as the principal. This will allow anonymous access on the bucket. Then, set a condition using the PrincipalOrgId key to limit the access to Organization members only.

Hence, the correct answer is: Declare a (*) wildcard as the principal and set PrincipalOrgId as a condition.

The option that says: Manually list all AWS member account as the principal is incorrect because it will take a lot of time to list all 200 account numbers into the S3 bucket policy. This solution is not efficient.

The option that says: Define PrincipalOrgId as the principal is incorrect because the principal key does not take PrincipalOrgId as a value.

The option that says: Declare the organization’s root account ID as the principal is incorrect because this will just give access to the root account and will not give access to all AWS accounts in the organization.

questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer needs to create Linux Amazon EC2 clusters on AWS. As per company policy for security groups of any EC2 instance, the SSH port should not be open to the public and should be configured to listen to a custom port.

How can the Engineer implement a monitoring system that automatically sends a notification when an instance does not follow the configured rules?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service automatically notifies when a security group allows SSH access to the public?
choices: Use an Amazon CloudWatch custom metric to check if a security group SSH port is open to the public, and then send a notification for non-compliance.
Use AWS Config and send a non-compliance notification when a security group SSH port is open to the public.
Use a combination of AWS Trusted Advisor and Amazon CloudWatch Alarm to flag a security group that has an SSH port open to the public.
Run a third-party scanning tool on your EC2 instances and have it generate a report of non-compliant instances.
correctAnswers: Use AWS Config and send a non-compliance notification when a security group SSH port is open to the public.
questions.explanation:
You can be notified when AWS Config evaluates your custom or managed rules against your resources. This is helpful if you want to ensure that your resources are compliant with a custom rule you defined. You can also configure AWS Config to stream configuration changes and notifications to an Amazon SNS topic. For example, when a resource is updated, you can get a notification sent to your email so that you can view the changes.

AWS Config

AWS Config sends notifications for the following events:

– Configuration item change for a resource. – Configuration history for a resource was delivered for your account. – Configuration snapshot for recorded resources was started and delivered for your account. – Compliance state of your resources and whether they are compliant with your rules. – Evaluation started for a rule against your resources. – AWS Config failed to deliver the notification to your account.

Hence, the correct answer is: Use AWS Config and send a non-compliance notification when a security group SSH port is open to the public.

The option that says: Use a combination of AWS Trusted Advisor and Amazon CloudWatch Alarm to flag a security group that has an SSH port open to the public is incorrect. Although AWS Trusted Advisor could flag if your security group for SSH is open to the public, it won’t flag a non-default SSH port that is open to the public. It is better to just use AWS Config instead.

The option that says: Use an Amazon CloudWatch custom metric to check if a security group SSH port is open to the public, and then send a notification for non-compliance is incorrect because AWS CloudWatch does not have this function. It can’t monitor or check your security group if it has a non-compliant inbound or outbound rule.

The option that says: Run a third-party scanning tool on your EC2 instances and have it generate a report of non-compliant instances is also incorrect. This option may be possible, but it will require you to use third-party tools, which may typically incur more work and costs for your company.

questions.question:
One of the Systems Administrators in your team has created a VPC with a CIDR block of 10.0.0.0/16 using the VPC wizard. There is a requirement to implement a hybrid cloud architecture in which you have to connect the client’s data center to its VPC in AWS. You created a public subnet with a CIDR block of (10.0.0.0/24) and a VPN-only subnet with a CIDR block of (10.0.1.0/24) along with the VPN gateway (vgw-51898) to connect to the data center, which has a CIDR block of 172.16.0.0/12. To allow traffic to the internet from the VPN subnet, you also made an additional setup for a NAT instance (i-918273).

Which of the following options is not a valid entry for the main route table in this scenario?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which route table entry is invalid for a VPC with a VPN-only subnet and NAT instance?
choices: Destination: 10.0.0.0/16 and Target: local
Destination: 172.16.0.0/12 and Target: vgw-51898
Destination: 0.0.0.0/0 and Target: i-918273
Destination: 10.0.1.0/24 and Target: i-918273
correctAnswers: Destination: 10.0.1.0/24 and Target: i-918273
questions.explanation:
The configuration for this scenario includes a virtual private cloud (VPC) with a public subnet and a private subnet, and a virtual private gateway to enable communication with your own network over an IPsec VPN tunnel. This is a recommended scenario if you want to extend your network into the cloud and also directly access the Internet from your VPC.

This scenario enables you to run a multi-tiered application with a scalable web front end in a public subnet, and to house your data in a private subnet that is connected to your network by an IPsec VPN connection.

In the main route table, the first entry is the default entry for local routing in the VPC; this entry enables the instances in the VPC to communicate with each other over IPv4. The second entry routes all other IPv4 subnet traffic from the private subnet to your network over the virtual private gateway (VPG).

The only routing option which should have access to the Internet gateway should be the 0.0.0.0/0 address. There should be no VPN-only subnet that routes to a NAT instance.

Hence, the correct answer is: Destination: 10.0.1.0/24 and Target: i-918273.

The option that says: Destination: 0.0.0.0/0 and Target: i-918273 is incorrect because this will allow all traffic from the internet to NAT instance which is a valid route.

The option that says: Destination: 172.16.0.0/12 and Target: vgw-51898 is incorrect because this is a valid route that will allow traffic from the on-premises data center to the virtual gateway.

The option that says: Destination: 10.0.0.0/16 and Target: local is incorrect because this is a default route used by resources to communicate within the VPC.

questions.question:
A company registered an account in AWS. After setting up their VPC and launching various AWS resources, you were instructed by the IT Security team to improve its overall security and performance. You must identify the most common security misconfigurations in your cloud resources for you to be able to address and resolve them immediately.

How will you check if you have followed the best practices in cloud security?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service identifies common security misconfigurations and recommends best practice improvements?
choices: Use AWS Config Security Checks to monitor and assess changes in the configurations of AWS resources.
Use AWS Inspector Checks to evaluate whether your assessment targets (your collection of AWS resources) have potential security issues that you need to address.
Use AWS Trusted Advisor Security Checks to inspect your AWS environment and make recommendations on different areas.
Use AWS X-Ray to analyze and debug applications to troubleshoot root causes of performance issues and errors.
correctAnswers: Use AWS Trusted Advisor Security Checks to inspect your AWS environment and make recommendations on different areas.
questions.explanation:
AWS Trusted Advisor is an online tool that provides you real-time guidance to help you provision your resources following AWS best practices. Trusted Advisor checks help optimize your AWS infrastructure, increase security and performance, reduce your overall costs, and monitor service limits. Whether establishing new workflows, developing applications, or as part of ongoing improvement, take advantage of the recommendations provided by Trusted Advisor on a regular basis to help keep your solutions provisioned optimally.

Trusted Advisor inspects your AWS environment and makes recommendations when opportunities may exist to save money, improve system performance, or close security gaps. It provides alerts on several of the most common security misconfigurations that can occur, including leaving certain ports open that make you vulnerable to hacking and unauthorized access, neglecting to create IAM accounts for your internal users, allowing public access to Amazon S3 buckets, not turning on user activity logging (AWS CloudTrail), or not using MFA on your root AWS Account.

Hence, the correct answer is: Use AWS Trusted Advisor Security Checks to inspect your AWS environment and make recommendations on different areas.

The option that says: Use AWS Config Security Checks to monitor and assess changes in the configurations of AWS resources is incorrect. AWS Config is a fully managed service that enables you to assess, audit, and evaluate the configurations of your AWS resources. This option is incorrect since it does not give you recommendations on what to check, unlike Trusted Advisor.

The option that says: Use AWS Inspector Checks to evaluate whether your assessment targets (your collection of AWS resources) have potential security issues that you need to address is incorrect because AWS Inspector assessment targets can consist only of EC2 instances that run on a number of supported operating systems. Therefore, it won’t be able to assess your other resources.

The option that says: Use AWS X-Ray to analyze and debug applications to troubleshoot root causes of performance issues and errors is incorrect because AWS X-Ray is only for analyzing and debugging applications to find causes of errors and performance issues. It does not provide any other insights into your non-application resources.

questions.question:
A financial firm is hosting its mission-critical system in AWS. The Lead CloudOps Engineer is responsible for properly monitoring the status of the cloud resources and setting up an alert system so that the operations team is notified of any technical issues. Since the system is critical to the day-to-day operations of the business, the CloudOps Engineer must also ensure that alerts are generated for any issues that occur in the underlying hardware hosting the AWS resources.

Which of the following is the best way to achieve this?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which service provides personalized alerts about AWS hardware issues affecting your specific resources?
choices: Set up a Service Health Dashboard that will automatically send alerts for any system issues.
Set up a custom monitoring tool using AWS CloudTrail that will send API requests to check the health of your AWS resources.
Simply use an Amazon CloudWatch Dashboard to automatically check the status of the underlying hardware that hosts your AWS resources and send alerts for any outages.
Use the Personal Health Dashboard, which provides information about AWS Health events that can affect your account.
correctAnswers: Use the Personal Health Dashboard, which provides information about AWS Health events that can affect your account.
questions.explanation:
AWS Personal Health Dashboard provides alerts and remediation guidance when AWS is experiencing events that may impact you. While the Service Health Dashboard displays the general status of AWS services, Personal Health Dashboard gives you a personalized view into the performance and availability of the AWS services underlying your AWS resources.

AWS Health Dashboard

The dashboard displays relevant and timely information to help you manage events in progress, and provides proactive notification to help you plan for scheduled activities. With Personal Health Dashboard, alerts are triggered by changes in the health of AWS resources, giving you event visibility, and guidance to help quickly diagnose and resolve issues.

Hence, the correct answer is: Use the Personal Health Dashboard, which provides information about AWS Health events that can affect your account.

The option that says: Simply use an Amazon CloudWatch Dashboard to automatically check the status of the underlying hardware that hosts your AWS resources and send alerts for any outages is incorrect because CloudWatch only monitors the health of the resources that you own based on certain metrics, but it does not check the underlying hardware that hosts the AWS resources.

The option that says: Set up a Service Health Dashboard that will automatically send alerts for any system issues is incorrect because the Service Health Dashboard monitors the entire health of the services provided by AWS on a global scale, and not just your account. Although this seems to be a valid option, take note that using the Personal Health Dashboard is a better option for this scenario.

The option that says: Set up a custom monitoring tool using AWS CloudTrail that will send API requests to check the health of your AWS resources is incorrect because CloudTrail is primarily used for API logging and not to check the health status of the AWS resources.

questions.question:
A bank utilizes AWS Firewall Manager for its multiple AWS accounts that are managed by AWS Organizations. The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) has instructed to change the default Firewall Manager administrator account to a different AWS account under the same organization. AWS Systems Manager Automation is used to manage the various AWS resources across multiple accounts and Regions.

Which of the following is the MOST suitable option to meet the requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What is the correct process to change the default Firewall Manager administrator account in an organization?
choices: Remove the existing Firewall Manager administrator AWS account from the organization. Add the new administrator AWS account to the organization to take its place within the same organization.

Modify the Service Control Policy (SCP) to restrict access to Firewall Manager in the current account, effectively prohibiting its usage. Adjust the SCP to enable access to Firewall Manager specifically for the new administrator AWS account and grant it the necessary permissions.

Sign in to the AWS account of the current Firewall Manager administrator. Utilize the revoke feature of Firewall Manager. Proceed to log into the AWS Management Console using the AWS Organizations management account. Input the AWS account number for the new Firewall Manager administrator.

Access the AWS Systems Manager console using the AWS Organizations member account. Switch the administrator account of Firewall Manager from the original administrator account to the new Firewall Manager administrator AWS account number.
correctAnswers: Sign in to the AWS account of the current Firewall Manager administrator. Utilize the revoke feature of Firewall Manager. Proceed to log into the AWS Management Console using the AWS Organizations management account. Input the AWS account number for the new Firewall Manager administrator.
questions.explanation:
AWS Firewall Manager is a security management service that allows you to centrally configure and manage firewall rules across your accounts and applications in AWS Organizations. As new applications are created, Firewall Manager makes it easier to bring new applications and resources into compliance by enforcing a common set of security rules.

Users can designate only one account in an organization as the default Firewall Manager administrator account. The default administrator account follows the principle of first in, last out.

To designate a different default administrator account, each individual administrator account must first revoke their own account. Then, the existing default administrator can revoke their own account, which also will offboard the organization from Firewall Manager. When an administrator revokes their account, all Firewall Manager policies created by that account are deleted. To designate a new default administrator account, you then must sign into Firewall Manager with the AWS Organizations management account to designate a new administrator account.

Hence, the correct answer is: Sign in to the AWS account of the current Firewall Manager administrator. Utilize the revoke feature of Firewall Manager. Proceed to log into the AWS Management Console using the AWS Organizations management account. Input the AWS account number for the new Firewall Manager administrator.

The option that says: Access the AWS Systems Manager console using the AWS Organizations member account. Switch the administrator account of Firewall Manager from the original administrator account to the new Firewall Manager administrator AWS account number is incorrect because the modification for the Firewall Manager account cannot be performed in the AWS Systems Manager console. In addition, a member account in the AWS Organizations service doesn’t have ample permissions to make any changes.

The option that says: Remove the existing Firewall Manager administrator AWS account from the organization. Add the new administrator AWS account to the organization to take its place within the same organization is incorrect because the Firewall Manager console can be used to remove the original Firewall Manager administrator account and to add the new Firewall Manager administrator account. You don’t have to entirely remove the current administrator AWS account from the organization.

The option that says: Modify the Service Control Policy (SCP) to restrict access to Firewall Manager in the current account, effectively prohibiting its usage. Adjust the SCP to enable access to Firewall Manager specifically for the new administrator AWS account and grant it the necessary permissions is incorrect because SCPs only allow or deny access to services from Organizations member accounts. SCPs do not control which account is the administrator account for Firewall Manager.

questions.question:
A multinational investment bank plans to adopt a hybrid cloud architecture. To migrate its on-premises applications to AWS Cloud, the CloudOps Engineer should prepare a couple of AWS CloudFormation templates that will automatically provision the required resources. The Engineer also creates an Amazon CloudWatch Synthetics role to monitor application endpoints after deployment.

Which section is required when designing a template?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which section is the only required component in an AWS CloudFormation template?
choices: Outputs section
Format Version section
Resources section
Parameters section
correctAnswers: Resources section
questions.explanation:
Templates include several major sections. The Resources section is the only required section. Some sections in a template can be in any order. However, as you build your template, it can be helpful to use the logical order shown in the following list because values in one section might refer to values from a previous section.

CloudFormation stack Resources section

Format Version (optional)

The AWS CloudFormation template version that the template conforms to. The template format version is not the same as the API or WSDL version. The template format version can change independently of the API and WSDL versions.

Description (optional)

A text string that describes the template. This section must always follow the template format version section.

Metadata (optional)

Objects that provide additional information about the template.

Parameters (optional)

Values to pass to your template at runtime (when you create or update a stack). You can refer to the parameters from the Resources and Outputs sections of the template.

Mappings (optional)

A mapping of keys and associated values that you can use to specify conditional parameter values, similar to a lookup table. You can match a key to a corresponding value by using the Fn::FindInMap intrinsic function in the Resources and Outputs section.

Conditions (optional)

Conditions that control whether certain resources are created or whether certain resource properties are assigned a value during stack creation or update. For example, you could conditionally create a resource that depends on whether the stack is for a production or test environment.

Transform (optional)

For serverless applications (also referred to as Lambda-based applications), specifies the version of the AWS Serverless Application Model (AWS SAM) to use. When you specify a transform, you can use AWS SAM syntax to declare resources in your template. The model defines the syntax that you can use and how it is processed. You can also use AWS::Include transforms to work with template snippets that are stored separately from the main AWS CloudFormation template. You can store your snippet files in an Amazon S3 bucket and then reuse the functions across multiple templates.

Resources (required)

Specifies the stack resources and their properties, such as an Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud instance or an Amazon Simple Storage Service bucket. You can refer to resources in the Resources and Outputs sections of the template.

Outputs (optional)

Describes the values that are returned whenever you view your stack’s properties. For example, you can declare an output for an S3 bucket name and then call the AWS CloudFormation describe-stacks AWS CLI command to view the name.

Hence, the correct answer is: Resources section.

As stated above, the following options are incorrect:

– Outputs section

– Format Version section

– Parameters section

questions.question:
A leading commercial bank is hosting its personal banking portal on a fleet of Amazon EC2 instances behind an Application Load Balancer. To reduce latency, an Amazon CloudFront distribution has been launched with the load balancer as its origin. The engineer also uses CloudFront invalidation to remove outdated cached content. The CloudOps Engineer received a report from the IT Security team regarding the discovery of numerous SQL injection attempts and cross-site scripting attacks on the online portal.

Which of the following services can help mitigate this attack?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service protects web applications from SQL injection and cross-site scripting attacks?
choices: AWS WAF
AWS Network Firewall
Origin Shield
Amazon VPC Traffic Mirroring
correctAnswers: AWS WAF
questions.explanation:
AWS WAF is a web application firewall that helps protect your web applications from common web exploits that could affect application availability, compromise security, or consume excessive resources.

AWS Web Application Firewall

AWS WAF gives you control over which traffic to allow or block to your web applications by defining customizable web security rules. You can use AWS WAF to create custom rules that block common attack patterns, such as SQL injection or cross-site scripting, and rules that are designed for your specific application.

Hence, the correct answer is: AWS WAF

Origin Shield is incorrect because this is just a centralized caching layer in Amazon CloudFront that helps increase your cache hit ratio to reduce the load on your origin. This feature is not capable of protecting your application from SQL Injection, XSS, and other web vulnerabilities.

Amazon VPC Traffic Mirroring is incorrect because this is an Amazon VPC feature that simply copies network traffic from the elastic network interfaces of your Amazon EC2 instances. This is primarily used for content inspection, threat monitoring, or network troubleshooting and not for mitigating web attacks.

AWS Network Firewall is incorrect because this is mainly used to perform deep packet inspection on the inbound traffic of your Amazon VPC. This service is specifically designed to identify potential hacking attempts, but not to actively protect your web applications from SQL Injection or XSS.

questions.question:
A company has numerous AWS accounts and is managed using AWS Organizations. The finance department has requested a monthly breakdown of cloud expenditures for each department. AWS Systems Manager OpsCenter is used as a centralized location to manage operational work items of the corporate AWS resources across AWS accounts.

What sequence of actions should the CloudOps Engineer take in order to supply the required information? (Select TWO.)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What two steps enable per-department cost reporting across all AWS accounts in an organization?
choices: Configure an AWS Config rule across all accounts in the organization to mark resources without a "Department" tag as non-compliant.

In the AWS Organizations management account, go to the AWS Billing and Cost Management console and activate the cost allocation tag named "Department". Utilize a tag policy to enforce a "Department" tag on newly created resources.
Utilize AWS Systems Manager Fleet Manager to identify resources lacking tags in each account. Apply a tag named "Department" to any resources that are untagged.
Utilize the AWS Resource Groups Tag Editor to identify resources lacking tags in each account. Apply a tag named "Department" to any resources that are untagged.
In each account, go to the AWS Billing and Cost Management console and activate the cost allocation tag named "Department".
correctAnswers: In the AWS Organizations management account, go to the AWS Billing and Cost Management console and activate the cost allocation tag named "Department". Utilize a tag policy to enforce a "Department" tag on newly created resources.
Utilize the AWS Resource Groups Tag Editor to identify resources lacking tags in each account. Apply a tag named "Department" to any resources that are untagged.
questions.explanation:
A tag is a label that users or AWS assigns to an AWS resource. Each tag consists of a key and a value. For each resource, each tag key must be unique, and each tag key can have only one value. Tags can be used to organize resources and cost allocation tags to track AWS costs on a detailed level.

After activating cost allocation tags, AWS uses the cost allocation tags to organize resource costs on the cost allocation report to make it easier to categorize and track AWS costs. AWS provides two types of cost allocation tags, AWS-generated tags, and user-defined tags. Both types of tags must be activated separately before they can appear in Cost Explorer or on a cost allocation report.

With Tag Editor, users can build a query to find resources in one or more AWS Regions that are available for tagging. Users can choose up to 20 individual resource types or build a query on All resource types. The query can include resources that already have tags or resources that have no tags.

Hence, the correct answers are:

– In the AWS Organizations management account, go to the AWS Billing and Cost Management console and activate the cost allocation tag named “Department”. Utilize a tag policy to enforce a “Department” tag on newly created resources.

– Utilize the AWS Resource Groups Tag Editor to identify resources lacking tags in each account. Apply a tag named “Department” to any resources that are untagged.

The option that says: Configure an AWS Config rule across all accounts in the organization to mark resources without a “Department” tag as non-compliant is incorrect because AWS Config rules are primarily used to evaluate the compliance of resources against predefined configuration policies. It does not provide financial or billing information in AWS.

The option that says: Utilize AWS Systems Manager Fleet Manager to identify resources lacking tags in each account. Apply a tag named “Department” to any resources that are untagged is incorrect because Fleet Manager is primarily used for providing performance insights and management capabilities for server fleets rather than resource tagging. It does not have the capability to identify untagged resources.

The option that says: In each account, go to the AWS Billing and Cost Management console and activate the cost allocation tag named “Department” is incorrect because cost allocation tags are managed at the management account level rather than being handled separately for each account. The management account acts as the central point for managing cost allocation tags, allowing for consistent tagging practices across multiple AWS accounts.

questions.question:
A FinTech startup is running a cryptocurrency exchange platform hosted in several Amazon EC2 instances behind an Application Load Balancer. The application stack is deployed using AWS CloudFormation, and each EC2 instance has a running AWS Systems Manager (SSM) agent. There was a recent incident in which the platform was down for a few hours because the instances had reached the maximum disk space allocated.

What should the CloudOps Engineer do to prevent this issue from happening again?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you monitor and alert on EC2 disk space utilization using CloudWatch and the SSM agent?
choices: Integrate AWS Health events with Amazon EventBridge to automatically filter and collect the disk space utilization data of all EC2 instances. Set up an Amazon SNS topic to notify the IT Operations team.

Use drift detection on the CloudFormation stack to automatically detect the specific EC2 instances that are running low on free disk space. Set up an Amazon CloudWatch alarm that will notify the IT Operations team if disk space is running low.

Download and install the Amazon CloudWatch agent on all EC2 Instances using the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) agent. Configure the CloudWatch agent to collect the disk metrics and set up a CloudWatch alarm that will notify the IT Operations team if disk space is running low.
Enable detailed monitoring in Amazon CloudWatch to track the disk space metrics of all EC2 instances. Set up an Amazon SNS topic to notify the IT Operations team.
correctAnswers: Download and install the Amazon CloudWatch agent on all EC2 Instances using the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) agent. Configure the CloudWatch agent to collect the disk metrics and set up a CloudWatch alarm that will notify the IT Operations team if disk space is running low.
questions.explanation:
Amazon CloudWatch is basically a metrics repository. An AWS service — such as Amazon EC2 —puts metrics into the repository, and you retrieve statistics based on those metrics. If you put your own custom metrics into the repository, you can retrieve statistics on these metrics as well.

You can use metrics to calculate statistics and then present the data graphically in the CloudWatch console. You can configure alarm actions to stop, start, or terminate an Amazon EC2 instance when certain criteria are met. In addition, you can create alarms that initiate Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling and Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) actions on your behalf.

Amazon CloudWatch

You can collect metrics from servers by installing the CloudWatch agent on the server. You can install the agent on both Amazon EC2 instances and on-premises servers and on servers running either Linux or Windows Server. If you install the agent on an Amazon EC2 instance, the metrics it collects are in addition to the metrics enabled by default on Amazon EC2 instances.

The CloudWatch agent can collect various metrics from the EC2 instance such as the total amount of memory that is currently in use (mem_used), the amount of memory that isn’t being used (mem_free) and many other metrics. You can install the CloudWatch Agent using the command line, the AWS Systems Manager, or via AWS CloudFormation.

Hence, the correct answer is: Download and install the Amazon CloudWatch agent on all EC2 Instances using the AWS Systems Manager (SSM) agent. Configure the CloudWatch agent to collect the disk metrics and set up a CloudWatch alarm that will notify the IT Operations team if disk space is running low.

The option that says: Use drift detection on the CloudFormation stack to automatically detect the specific EC2 instances that are running low on free disk space. Set up an Amazon CloudWatch alarm that will notify the IT Operations team if disk space is running low is incorrect because drift detection is just a feature of CloudFormation that enables you to detect whether a stack’s actual configuration differs, or has drifted, from its expected configuration. It is not suitable for use in monitoring the disk space of your EC2 instances.

The option that says: Enable detailed monitoring in Amazon CloudWatch to track the disk space metrics of all EC2 instances. Set up an Amazon SNS topic to notify the IT Operations team is incorrect because the detailed monitoring feature in CloudWatch simply increases the frequency of sending metric data to CloudWatch in 1-minute periods. The disk space metrics would still be unavailable even if you enabled this feature.

The option that says: Integrate AWS Health events with Amazon EventBridge to automatically filter and collect the disk space utilization data of all EC2 instances. Set up an Amazon SNS topic to notify the IT Operations team is incorrect because AWS Health just provides ongoing visibility into the state of your AWS resources, services, and accounts. The AWS Health only tracks the availability of the services that you are using and doesn’t go into the details of checking the disk space utilization data. A better solution here is to download and install the CloudWatch agent instead.
